title: Understanding Moment of Inertia
text: The moment of inertia is a measure of an object's resistance to changes in its rotation. It depends on the mass distribution relative to the axis of rotation.

title: Formula for Moment of Inertia
text: For a cylindrical object, the moment of inertia can be expressed as $$I=k m r^2$$, where k is a constant that depends on the shape of the object.

title: Finding the Constant k
text: The constant k can be determined by analyzing the motion of the object as it rolls down the incline. Consider the forces and torques acting on the object.

title: Calculate the Moment of Inertia
text: Using the given information and the formula $$I=k m r^2$$, calculate the moment of inertia of the body.
